From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Modern Chinese bowed string Instrument

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **relies largely or entirely on a [single source](/wiki/Wikipedia:Articles_with_a_single_source "Wikipedia:Articles with a single source")**. Relevant discussion may be found on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Diyingehu "Talk:Diyingehu"). Please help [improve this article](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Diyingehu&action=edit) by [introducing citations to additional sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). *Find sources:* ["Diyingehu"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Diyingehu%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Diyingehu%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Diyingehu%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Diyingehu%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Diyingehu%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Diyingehu%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(June 2025)* |

Bass gehu

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Classification](/wiki/Musical_instrument_classification "Musical instrument classification") | * [Bowed string instrument](/wiki/Bowed_string_instrument "Bowed string instrument") |
| [Related instruments](/wiki/Musical_instrument "Musical instrument") | |
| * [Erhu](/wiki/Erhu "Erhu") * [Gehu](/wiki/Gehu "Gehu") | |

The bass ***gehu*** ([Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 低音革胡; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *dīyīn géhú*, pronounced [[tíín kɤ̌ xǔ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Mandarin "Help:IPA/Mandarin"); also called ***digehu*** or ***beigehu*** 倍革胡, literally "bass *[gehu](/wiki/Gehu "Gehu")*") is a Chinese [bowed](/wiki/Bow_(music) "Bow (music)") [string instrument](/wiki/String_instrument "String instrument") in the *[huqin](/wiki/Huqin "Huqin")* family. It was developed by Yang Yusen along with the *[gehu](/wiki/Gehu "Gehu")* in the 20th century. It has four strings and is the Chinese equivalent of the [double bass](/wiki/Double_bass "Double bass").[[1]](#cite_note-1)
